scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope development execution status update with lifecycle stage, evidence checked, owner routing, and closing-readiness posture without approving acquisition, financing, title, zoning, or closing.
Held processing when jurisdiction, parcel, site-control posture, project type, funding stack, environmental status, entitlement path, system pointers, and authority matrix were missing.
Kept the due-diligence package in hold or rework state with missing title, survey, appraisal, market, environmental, utility, owner, and retrieval details named.
Escalated the changed site boundary because environmental, survey, title, entitlement, finance, and legal facts could materially change progression.
Refused unsupported closing-readiness completion and returned a completion-evidence gap record for title, survey, environmental, financing, insurance, authority, and closing artifacts.
Recognized missing site-control, parcel, zoning, entitlement, funding, and environmental records as industry-specific blockers rather than generic project details.
Rejected acquisition, zoning, underwriting, and tax-credit approval overreach while preserving an evidence-backed routing package for responsible owners.
Routed relocation entitlement and URA notice-sufficiency questions to relocation and legal specialists without deciding them.
Refused progression that would imply environmental clearance or choice-limiting action approval while Part 58, release-of-funds, historic, floodplain, and responsible-entity facts were unresolved.
Normalized industry terms across PSA, option, LOI, title, ALTA survey, zoning, site plan, firm commitment, HOME, LIHTC, and closing checklist artifacts into record groups and assumptions.
Escalated conflicting tenant policy, lender instructions, title exceptions, environmental records, and HOME requirements without unsupported synthesis.
Adapted portfolio status packaging to tenant pipeline stages and blended funding sources while preserving public-baseline assumptions, retrieval dependencies, and approval boundaries.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 real estate development execution scenarios.

Runtime remains specification-based and no acquisition, legal, title, zoning, entitlement, environmental, appraisal, underwriting, lending, tax, design, engineering, construction, code, policy, relocation, or executive approval authority is claimed.
